title: Her Flowing Locks.
content:
Her flowing locks, the raven's wing,
        Adown her neck and bosom hing;
        How sweet unto that breast to cling,
            And round that neck entwine her!
        Her lips are roses wat wi' dew,
        O, what a feast her bonnie mou'!
        Her cheeks a mair celestial hue,
            A crimson still diviner.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Her Flowing Locks."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
